meaning: n.女歌女(尤指)女民谣歌手
examples: I'm also a social anthropologist, diseuse, chanteuse, children's book illustrator.
En route, Dan meets chanteuse Lenore Brent, truly his kind of woman.
The fading chanteuse, he wrote, was “like a dying moth, ” singing “in a voice like a shout from a tomb, . . . the voice of life itself, refusing to die, refusing to be silenced.”
Into this minefield of potential resentment and painful rancour, stepped a slight, soft-voiced chanteuse.
But Makeba fiercely resisted being pigeonholed as a musician, describing herself simply as a chanteuse, a singer.
